title: Floral Dense Turkish Poppy Seed Fritters
description: A fragrant, cake-like fritter bursting with ground poppy seeds and subtle rosewater, served hot with a dusting of powdered sugar.

ingredients.name: ground poppy seeds
ingredients.notes: finely ground for a smooth texture

instructions: In a medium bowl, combine flour, ground poppy seeds, sugar, yeast, and salt.
Create a small well in the center and pour in warm water and olive oil; stir until a dough forms.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let the dough rise in a warm spot for 45 minutes, or until it doubles in size.
While the dough rises, whisk the egg and set aside.
Once risen, punch down the dough and transfer it to a lightly floured surface; knead for 2 minutes until smooth.
Use a tablespoon to scoop small portions of dough and place them on a parchment-lined tray; cover again and let rest for 10 minutes to allow the batter to thicken slightly.
Heat melted butter in a nonstick skillet over medium heat; once hot, drop one batter portion at a time into the skillet.
Fry each fritter for 3-4 minutes per side, or until golden brown and puffed; do not overcrowd the pan.
Remove fritters with a slotted spoon and lay them on paper towels to drain excess butter.
Set aside a few warm fritters for serving; dust the rest lightly with powdered sugar before plating.
Serve immediately with a drizzle of rose water or a dollop of whipped cream for an extra show‑off touch.
